授業目的: この科目の目的は、ミクロ経済学の基礎である供給、需要、消費者行動、企業行動について説明することです。履修する際には、基礎的な数学知識が望ましいです。
到達目標: この科目を履修することで、受講生は以下のことができるようになります：1. 消費者行動を理解する 2. 企業行動を理解する 3. 経済における政府の役割を理解する4. 労働市場の機能を理解する
授業の概要・背景: 原則として、ハーバード大学教授N.グレゴリー・マンキウの「ミクロ経済学の原理」という教科書に沿った授業を行います。まず、需要と供給、つまり消費者の行動、生産者の行動、市場の均衡を決定する要因について学びます。次に、この知識を踏まえて、企業行動、公共部門と労働市場について様々な分析を行います。
授業方法:
Each class consists of a lecture (about 60-70 minutes) and a practical exercise (about 20-30 minutes). The exercise can be done individually or in groups. We begin by studying supply and demand - the forces that determine consumer behavior, producer
 behavior and market equilibrium. We then analyze firm behavior, the public sector and the labor market.

教科書/Required texts0: There is no required textbook. However, the class will mainly follow the contents of Harvard University Professor N. Gregory Mankiw’s “Principles of Microeconomics” textbook.
参考書/Reference books2: 小川 光 、 家森 信善
ベーシック＋（プラス）／ミクロ経済学の基礎
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: This class will follow the contents of Harvard University Professor N. Gregory Mankiw’s “Principles of Microeconomics” textbook. However, purchasing the textbook is not required.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Specialized Foundation Courses:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.

成績評価Grading4: 備考　Note Evaluation is mostly based on weekly quizzes and in-class participation (around 60%). The remainder is a 30%  take-home final exam, which is based on the content of weekly quizzes, and 10% for in-class participation. In order to successfully complete the quizzes and pass the final exam, class attendance is essential.
